ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: I tend to divide Filipinos in America into two types: the FOBs (fresh-off-the-boat/newly arrived immigrants) and the Filipino Americans.
There are situations where I feel inferior because of my ethnic/cultural background.
I find persons with lighter skin-tones to be more attractive than persons with dark skin-tones.
In general, I do not associate with newly-arrived Filipino immigrants.
I do not want my children to be dark-skinned.
Filipinos should feel privileged and honored that Spain and the United States had contact with them.
There are situations where I feel that it is more advantageous or necessary to deny my ethnic/cultural heritage.
The American ways of living or the American culture is generally more admirable, desirable, or better than the Filipino culture.
There are situations where I feel ashamed of my ethnic/cultural background.
I generally think that a person that is part white and part Filipino is more attractive than a full-blooded Filipino.
I believe that Filipino Americans are superior, more admirable, and more civilized than Filipinos in the Philippines.
In general, I am ashamed of newly arrived Filipino immigrants because of the way they dress and act.
I find persons who have bridged noses (like Whites) as more attractive than persons with Filipino noses.
I generally do not like newly-arrived Filipino immigrants.
I would like to have a skin-tone that is lighter than the skin-tone I have.
I think newly arrived immigrants should become as Americanized as quickly as possible.
I would like to have children with light skin-tones.
Spain and the United States are highly responsible for civilizing Filipinos and improving their ways of life.
I think newly-arrived immigrant Filipinos are backwards, have accents, and act weird.
I would like to have a nose that is more bridged (like Whites) than the nose I have.
Filipinos should be thankful to Spain and the United States for transforming the Filipino ways of life into a White/European American ways of life.
I tend to pay more attention to the opinions of Filipinos who are very Americanized than to the opinions of FOBs/newly-arrived immigrants.
In general, Filipino Americans should be thankful and feel fortunate for being in the United States.
In general, I feel that being a Filipino American is not as good as being White/European American.
I do not want my children to have Filipino noses.
In general, Filipino Americans do not have anything to complain about because they are lucky to be in the United States.
I feel that there are very few things about the Filipino culture that I can be proud of.
The colonization of the Philippines by Spain and the United States produced very little damage to the Filipino culture.
In general, I feel that being Filipino is a curse.
In general, I am ashamed of newly-arrived Filipino immigrants because of their inability to speak fluent, accent-free English.
In general, I am embarrassed of the Filipino culture and traditions.
In general, I make fun of, tease, or badmouth Filipinos who are not very Americanized in their behaviors.
There are moments when I wish I was a member of a ethnic/cultural group that is different from my own.
I make fun of, tease, or badmouth Filipinos who speak English with strong accents.
In general, I feel ashamed of the Filipino culture and traditions.
In general, I feel that being a person of my ethnic/cultural background is not as good as being White.
